q.en: According to the book, where did the game of lacrosse begin?
q.zh: 根据本书，长曲棍球这项运动起源于哪里？

options.en: In the St. Lawrence River Valley in present-day Canada
options.zh: 今加拿大境内的圣劳伦斯河谷

options.en: In the stadiums of ancient Greece
In the northeastern United States in the 1870s
In Australia in the 1900s

explain.en: The book states, 'Lacrosse started in the St. Lawrence River Valley in present-day Canada. It was played by First Nations (Native) people in the region.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'长曲棍球起源于今加拿大境内的圣劳伦斯河谷。它由当地的原住民（第一民族）游玩。'

q.zh: 在长曲棍球中，cradling（抱球摆动）是什么？

options.en: The back-and-forth motion of the crosse head that keeps the ball in place while a player runs
options.zh: 球棍头前后摆动、在奔跑时让球保持在位上的动作

options.en: A type of goal scored from far away
The face-off at the center of the field
A special helmet worn by goalies

explain.en: The book explains, 'Cradling is the back-and-forth motion of a crosse head that keeps the ball in place while a player runs.'
explain.zh: 书中解释：'cradling 是球棍头前后摆动、在奔跑时让球保持在位上的动作。'

q.en: Who is called the 'father of modern lacrosse'?
q.zh: 谁被称为'现代长曲棍球之父'？

options.en: George Beers, who wrote down the rules in 1860
options.zh: 乔治·比尔斯，他在1860年写下了规则

options.en: Oren Lyons, a famous goalkeeper
options.zh: 奥伦·莱昂斯，著名的守门员

options.en: Jim Brown, a football legend
A French settler who first watched the game
options.zh: 第一位观看这项运动的法国定居者

explain.en: The book says, 'In 1860, a man named George Beers wrote down the rules of the game... Beers is called the "father of modern lacrosse."'
explain.zh: 书中说：'1860年，一位叫乔治·比尔斯的人写下了比赛规则……比尔斯被称为"现代长曲棍球之父"。'

q.en: How many players are on the field for a men's lacrosse team?
q.zh: 一支男子长曲棍球队在场上共有多少名球员？

options.en: Ten
Twelve
Fifteen
Twenty-two

explain.en: The book states, 'Each men's team has ten players on the field. Three attackers... three defenders... three midfielders... a goalkeeper.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'每支男子队在场上共有十名球员：三名前锋、三名后卫、三名中场，以及一名守门员。'

q.en: In the glossary, what is a 'reservation'?
q.zh: 词汇表中，reservation（保留地）是什么？

options.en: Land set aside by the government for Native Americans
options.zh: 政府为原住民划定的土地

options.en: A place where lacrosse games are played indoors
options.zh: 室内进行长曲棍球比赛的场地

options.en: A type of curved lacrosse stick
options.zh: 一种弯曲的长曲棍球棍

options.en: A trophy given at the World Championship
options.zh: 世界锦标赛颁发的奖杯

explain.en: The glossary defines reservation as 'land set aside by government for Native Americans (p. 13).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 reservation 定义为'政府为原住民划定的土地（第13页）'。

q.en: In which years was lacrosse included in the Summer Olympics?
q.zh: 长曲棍球曾在哪两年进入夏季奥运会？

options.en: It has never been in the Olympics

explain.en: The book notes, 'Lacrosse was included in the Summer Olympics twice, in 1904 and 1908.'
explain.zh: 书中提到：'长曲棍球曾两次进入夏季奥运会，分别是1904年和1908年。'

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To explain what lacrosse is and compare it with other popular sports
options.zh: 解释长曲棍球是什么，并把它与其他流行运动做比较

options.en: To persuade readers to become professional athletes
options.zh: 说服读者成为职业运动员

options.en: To teach the history of Canada only
To argue that lacrosse should be in the Olympics
options.zh: 主张长曲棍球应进入奥运会

explain.en: The Focus Question is 'How is lacrosse similar to and different from other popular sports?' and the text compares it to basketball, soccer, and hockey, showing an informative, compare-and-contrast purpose.
explain.zh: 本书的焦点问题是'长曲棍球与其他流行运动有哪些相同和不同？'，并把这项运动与篮球、足球、冰球比较，体现介绍与对比的目的。

q.en: In the glossary, what does 'exceed' mean?
q.zh: 词汇表中，exceed（超过）是什么意思？

options.en: To go beyond
To catch a ball with the hands
To play indoors
To become famous

explain.en: The glossary defines exceed as 'to go beyond (p. 12).'
explain.zh: 词汇表将 exceed 定义为'超出、超过（第12页）'。

q.en: Which player does the book call possibly the greatest female lacrosse player ever?
q.zh: 书中称谁可能是史上最优秀的女子长曲棍球运动员？

options.en: Jen Adams, an Australian who played college lacrosse
options.zh: 詹·亚当斯，一位打过大学长曲棍球的澳大利亚人

options.en: Oren Lyons
Jim Brown
George Beers

explain.en: The book says Jen Adams 'broke many records and many people think she is the greatest female lacrosse player ever.'
explain.zh: 书中说詹·亚当斯'打破了许多纪录，许多人认为她是有史以来最优秀的女子长曲棍球运动员'。

q.en: Why did lacrosse start to grow in popularity in the United States after the 1970s?
q.zh: 为什么20世纪70年代以后长曲棍球在美国开始流行起来？

options.en: Cheaper lacrosse sticks made of lightweight metal became available
options.zh: 更便宜的轻金属球棍开始上市

options.en: The rules were written down in 1860
It was added to the Olympics
Schools were required to teach it

explain.en: The book says, 'Until the 1970s, lacrosse sticks were handmade only by certain First Nations tribes. When cheaper sticks made of lightweight metal hit the market, lacrosse started to take off.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'直到20世纪70年代，球棍还只由某些第一民族部落手工制作。当更便宜的轻金属球棍上市，长曲棍球开始迅速流行。'

prompt.en: The book says successful lacrosse players need certain characteristics. Write a short paragraph naming three important traits (such as speed, teamwork, or bravery) and give one fact from the book to support each.
prompt.zh: 书中说成功的长曲棍球运动员需要某些特质。写一小段话，列出三个重要特质（如速度、团队合作或勇敢），并用书中一个事实支持每一点。

en: Look at the 'Famous Players' and 'A Spectacular Game' sections for examples.
Use the book's facts, like goal shots exceeding 100 mph or players' bravery.
zh: 查阅'著名球员'和'壮观的比赛'两节寻找例子。
运用书中事实，如射门速度超过每小时100英里或球员的勇敢。
